channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: Israel’s defense minister says that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, say he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: UK Supreme Court to rule on landmark legal challenge over legal definition of a woman
item.description: The U.K. Supreme Court is set to decide a case on the definition of a woman in a long-running dispute between a women’s rights group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mozambique carried out a brutal crackdown on post-election protests, Amnesty says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Mozambique's security forces carried out a brutal, three-month crackdown on protesters after the country's election last year, a leading international rights group said Wednesday, citing local activists who alleged that more than 300 people were killed and more than 3,
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab And Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France said Tuesday it was expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US blasts UN for extending human rights envoy to Palestinian territories
item.description: The United States is rebuking a United Nations decision to extend Francesca Albanese’s tenure as the U.N. special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Another US aircraft carrier in Mideast waters ahead of second round of Iran-US nuclear talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A second U.S. aircraft carrier is operating in Mideast waters ahead of the next round of talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program, satellite photos analyzed Tuesday by The Associated Press showed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Hamas terror leader killed in 'precise strike' by Israel: IDF
item.description: Top Hamas leader Muhammad al-‘Ajlah has been killed in an Israeli strike, the IDF has announced. Al-'Ajilah was responsible for arming terrorists with weapons used to carry out attacks against Israeli civilians and IDF troops, according to the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The first lawsuit is filed over the deadly nightclub roof collapse in the Dominican Republic
item.description: The first known lawsuit has been filed against the owners of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed and killed 231 people in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Martín Adames, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hungary passes constitutional amendment to ban LGBTQ+ public events, seen as a major blow to rights
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary’s parliament on Monday passed an amendment to the constitution that allows the government to ban public events by LGBTQ+ communities, a decision that legal scholars and critics call another step toward authoritarianism by the populist government.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singapore dissolves parliament, paving the way for May 3 general election
item.description: Singapore’s parliament was dissolved Tuesday, paving the way for general elections in which the city-state’s long-ruling People’s Action Party will seek to strengthen its dominance under Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US flies long-range bomber for drill with South Korea, as the North marks a key state anniversary
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The United States flew long-range B-1B bombers in a show of force against North Korea on Tuesday, days after the powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to resist a U.S.-led push to eliminate the North’s nuclear program. North Korea often reacts to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yoon's trial on rebellion charges opens in South Korea. Here's what to know
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol's legal saga is far from over. Ten days after he was ousted from office over his martial law declaration, the former conservative South Korean president appeared for the first time at his criminal trial Monday on high-stakes rebellion charges.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti's government adopts a 'war budget' as town after town falls to gangs
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Haiti’s government announced Monday that it approved what it called a “war budget” of $275,000 aimed at alleviating the country’s crisis as gang violence surges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Attack by gunmen leaves at least 40 people dead in Nigeria, the country's president says
item.description: Nigeria’s president says that at least 40 people were killed when Muslim gunmen, believed to be herders, attacked a Christian farming community in the north-central part of the country, the latest in a spate of increasing violence in the West African country.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Britain's 'Stop Brexit Man' acquitted by judge over his musical protests outside Parliament
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An anti- Brexit activist who has spent years mounting a one-man protest outside Britain’s Parliament won a court victory on Monday against a police attempt to pull the plug on his musical activities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis makes appearance after Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still recovering from double pneumonia, but the 88-year-old still met with faithful after the Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican on April 13, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ballerina Ksenia Karelina freed from Russian custody in prisoner exchange
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
